name: How much does it usually cost to replace all the siding on a house in Pacifica or Daly City?
headline: How much does it usually cost to replace all the siding on a house in Pacifica or Daly City?

mainEntity.name: How much does it usually cost to replace all the siding on a house in Pacifica or Daly City?
mainEntity.text: How much does it usually cost to replace all the siding on a house in Pacifica or Daly City?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Bay Area, a full siding replacement on an average single‑family home often runs from **$15,000 to $45,000+**, but the range is wide because houses and materials vary a lot. Here’s what mainly drives cost: - Material: Fiber‑cement and engineered wood usually cost more than basic vinyl but less than real cedar or stucco with foam. Thicker, premium products and custom colors add cost. - House shape: More corners, multiple stories, and lots of windows and trim all add labor. - Prep work: Tearing off old siding, repairing rot, adding sheathing or insulation, and bringing flashing up to code can add thousands. - Access: Steep lots, tight side yards, or three‑story walls need more scaffolding and time. For a small one‑story house with simple walls and minimal rot, you might see total costs in the mid‑teens. A larger two‑story home in Hillsborough or San Bruno with complex architecture can easily go over $40k, especially with high‑end materials and upgraded trim. Pitfalls to avoid: - Choosing the lowest bid without checking exactly what’s included (housewrap, flashing, trim, painting). - Ignoring hidden damage in the budget—many older coastal homes have some rot once the old siding comes off. - Not asking about permit costs and inspections. Always ask for a written scope and line‑item estimate so you know what you’re paying for.
